# EVM Calculator

> EVM Calculator is a free, single-page web tool that computes all standard Earned Value Management metrics from four inputs (BAC, PV, EV, AC). It is designed for project managers, cost engineers, and PMP candidates who need fast, accurate calculations of cost and schedule performance with formulas and plain-English explanations.

The tool calculates eight metrics in real time: Cost Performance Index (CPI), Schedule Performance Index (SPI), Cost Variance (CV), Schedule Variance (SV), Estimate at Completion (EAC), Estimate to Complete (ETC), Variance at Completion (VAC), and To-Complete Performance Index (TCPI). All calculations happen locally in the browser; no data is sent to any server.

## Pages

- [Home — EVM Calculator](https://evm-calculator.replit.app/): The main calculator. Enter BAC, PV, EV, and AC and instantly see all eight EVM metrics with formulas, color-coded status indicators, and a glossary.
- [CPI Calculator](https://evm-calculator.replit.app/cpi-calculator/): Focused single-metric calculator for Cost Performance Index from EV and AC, with worked example, FAQ, and HowTo schema.
- [EAC Calculator](https://evm-calculator.replit.app/eac-calculator/): Focused calculator for Estimate at Completion using BAC, EV, and AC, with all three EAC formulas, FAQ, and HowTo schema.
- [PMP Formulas Cheat Sheet](https://evm-calculator.replit.app/pmp-formulas/): Printable reference of all standard EVM formulas for the PMP exam.
- [What is Budget at Completion?](https://evm-calculator.replit.app/what-is-budget-at-completion/): Long-form guide to BAC, including how it is set, how it differs from EAC, and common pitfalls.
- [About](https://evm-calculator.replit.app/about/): Background on the calculator, who built it, and why it exists.
- [Privacy Policy](https://evm-calculator.replit.app/privacy-policy/): Privacy practices. The calculator does not collect personal data; all calculations are client-side.
- [Terms of Service](https://evm-calculator.replit.app/terms-of-service/): Terms covering educational use, no warranty, and limitation of liability.

## Author and trust

The EVM Calculator is an independent educational tool. It cites the Project Management Institute's "Standard for Earned Value Management" as the formula source. PMP and PMBOK are PMI trademarks; this site is not affiliated with PMI. Contact: contact@evm-calculator.replit.app.

## Key formulas

- CPI = EV / AC
- SPI = EV / PV
- CV  = EV - AC
- SV  = EV - PV
- EAC = BAC / CPI
- ETC = EAC - AC
- VAC = BAC - EAC
- TCPI = (BAC - EV) / (BAC - AC)

## Interpretation

- Indices (CPI, SPI, TCPI) >= 1.0 indicate on-track or favorable performance; < 1.0 indicates unfavorable.
- Variances (CV, SV, VAC) >= 0 indicate favorable; < 0 indicates unfavorable.
- When a denominator is zero or an input is missing, the calculator displays "N/A" instead of an invalid number.
